trigger: User challenged the claimed repaired nose-mask pass, noting that the mask appears to cover the mouth and does not line up with the actual nose.
supersedes: the pass-with-notes semantic alignment fields in Plan/Instructions/QA/Evidence/Mask_Factory/Wave70/mf70_nose.json as written before this dispute
corrected_findings: The repaired mask still rides too low into the philtrum/upper-lip area and is visually too close to the mouth boundary for strict protected-neighbor pass.
The mask boundary is still a coarse polygonal approximation rather than an anatomically faithful full visible nose boundary.
Generated-output stability does not prove mask alignment and must not be used to promote this mask.
The tracker/item CSV rows were already still set to Mask_Alignment_Fail_Generated_Output_Safe_Target_Runtime_Pending and should remain failed until a visibly correct overlay is produced.
required_fix: Regenerate mf70_nose with a visibly anatomical nose boundary, not a shortcut trapezoid/polygon.
Keep clear separation from philtrum, upper lip, mouth, inner canthus, lower eyelids, and broad cheeks.
Use zoomed source/overlay review as the gate before any generated-output proof.
Do not mark pass unless the overlay itself, without considering generated-output stability, matches the named full_nose_bridge_tip_nostrils target.
